items.title: Spirit of Tasmania operator to get $506m government bailout amid bungled ferry rollout

children.content: The announcement comes as TT-Line continues to wrestle with the financial fallout from its bungled delivery of the new Spirit of Tasmania vessels.

gallery.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

thumbnail.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

image.altText: Images of an abandoned station in the icy and snowy Antarctic wilderness.

items.title: 'Different doctor every fortnight' is the reality for these residents

children.content: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.

image.altText: A middle aged man wearing a hat and his mother outside a medical clinic

items.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Liberals in promise management

children.content: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff smiles.

items.title: 'A good fit': How ex-Lambie senator found a home with Labor

children.content: She won office running under Jacqui Lambie's banner, but brand-new Labor recruit Tasmanian senator Tammy Tyrrell says she wanted a "seat at the powerful table of the government".

image.altText: Tammy Tyrrell

items.title: TassieCast: Is Tasmania facing a cut to arts?

children.content: The state government has already chopped a number of creative courses from TasTAFE, leading to fears in the arts industry of a state-wide creative brain drain. And as the state budget looms, there are more worries. Airlie Ward chats with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: 'Pre-existing dispute' likely reason for shooting, Tasmania police say

children.content: Police say a shooting yesterday involved people "known to police" and that it "stems from a pre-existing dispute between those two individuals".

image.altText: Police standing on a residential street.

items.title: After long absence, Tasmania's short-stay figures just got released

children.content: Almost 500 dwellings in Tasmania have been converted to short-stay accommodation in just over a year. It's the first time short-stay data has been updated since the end of 2024.

image.altText: Woman opens a door with a key

items.title: Angus Taylor promises permanent tax cuts and to reinstate all negative gearing

children.content: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

articles.title: Taylor 'huffing and puffing on the dog whistle': Butler

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler says the group Australia repatriated from the hantavirus cruise ship are in "good shape" ahead of their arrival in Perth today. Follow live.

articles.title: Finally, a tax fight that actually means something

children.content: Labor and the Liberals are offering imperfect but undeniably bold alternative visions for the tax system. It's nice to have something to talk about.

image.altText: Angus Taylor and Anthony Albanese in a composite image. Both are in suits

articles.title: Sound and dubious policy in budget reply

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor commits to phasing in income tax indexation under a "tax back guarantee" that he says "will fully protect all taxpayers from inflation".

items.title: Why millennials and gen Z feel ripped off by this budget

children.content: The prime minister said this budget was about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.

image.altText: A 'sold' sticker being placed on a 'for auction' sign outside a house.

items.title: Trust tax could hurt young, not just ultra-wealthy, tax advisors warn

children.content: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

items.title: Delta Goodrem reaches Eurovision grand final after fiery performance

children.content: Delta Goodrem has performed her pop ballad Eclipse at Eurovision in Vienna, the 11th time Australia has appeared in the musical extravaganza.

items.title: ASX opens higher as Wall Street rallies with records

children.content: The ASX opens higher in morning trading after rallies on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Vaccine program slashes RSV hospitalisations of young babies

children.content: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

image.altText: A young mother with long blonde hair holds up a smiling baby boy

items.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'

children.content: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

image.altText: Portrait of Yoorrook Commissioner Sue-Anne Hunter

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA

children.content: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Talbot Bay tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.

thumbnail.altText: A jetski on a big wave

items.title: Overdose at Crown Melbourne every two weeks, data shows

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: Matthew Wale becomes Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot win

children.content: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

items.title: Archaeologists restoring oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia

children.content: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

items.title: 'Mega' gas-fired power stations could be the new normal, analyst says

items.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch
